title: ERC4337 validateUserOp with branchless validationData encoding and missingFunds prefund forwarding
explanation: Three optimizations: (1) validationData is encoded branchlessly as iszero(success) — exactly 0 on valid, 1 on invalid, with the validUntil/validAfter bits left at 0 to mean indefinite validity. The full bit layout (success | validUntil<<160 | validAfter<<208) is degenerate when both time bounds are zero. (2) The prefund forwarding uses codesize() as input/output offset for the call — codesize is a non-zero memory pointer that's never dereferenced because input/output length is 0. Saves loading a 0 constant. (3) Failure of the prefund call is intentionally ignored (pop) because the EntryPoint already enforces the prefund accounting; reverting here would only block edge cases where the EntryPoint already has sufficient deposit.
when_to_apply: Any ERC4337 v0.7 smart account with simple signature-only validation
when_not_to_apply: Accounts with time-bounded validity (validUntil/validAfter must be encoded), accounts with paymaster integration requiring richer validationData
